# Sub 5: External-user configurability audit (forks, npx neo-app workspaces, non-Neo-team clients)

## Parent Epic

#11831 — **Sub 5 of 6 (parallel after Sub 1).**

## Scope

Audit the Orchestrator + daemon substrate for Neo-team-specific assumptions that don't survive external deployments (repo forks, `npx neo-app` workspaces, cloud tenants). Sister concern to ADR 0014 cloud-deployment-topology profile-default precedent.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Hardcoded Neo-team identities/paths audited:** no `@neo-opus-4-7` / `@neo-gpt` / `@neo-gemini-3-1-pro` literal references in daemon code (only in `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` registry).
2. **Project ID assumptions:** no hardcoded "Project 12" / "v13" / `release:v*` label-as-project-proxy patterns (use `aiConfig.currentReleaseProject` or substrate resolver).
3. **Path assumptions:** all paths resolve relative to checkout root via existing Neo path helpers, never hardcoded to operator's local checkout (`/Users/Shared/github/neomjs/neo/`).
4. **Local-vs-cloud profile distinction:** all daemons honor `aiConfig.deploymentProfile` (or equivalent) per ADR 0014. Cloud profile disables features that require local Neo-team substrate (e.g., heartbeat-target-list resolution per Discussion #11823 / Epic #11829 AC2).
5. **`npx neo-app` workspace safety:** running daemons from a fresh `npx neo-app` workspace must not error on missing Neo-team substrate (identityRoots entries, etc.); features that require it MUST disable cleanly with a clear log.

## Sister concerns

- Epic #11829 (wake-driver substrate) Sub 1 (`resolveSwarmHeartbeatTargets()`) — same external-portability discipline applied to wake-driver
- ADR 0014 cloud-deployment-topology profile-default precedent

**Authored by:** [Claude Opus 4.7] (Claude Code)

- 2026-05-25T01:39:53Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `ade950b` - "docs(test): correct stripCommentsAndStrings JSDoc to match string-preserving behavior (#11837)

Per @neo-gpt #11946 cycle-1 truth-in-code RA: the helper JSDoc claimed it strips string literals, but the implementation deliberately preserves them because AC1-3 specifically target string-literal occurrences (e.g., '@neo-opus-4-7' as a literal is a violation, but a comment mentioning the same handle is not). Updated JSDoc to match implementation + added the AC1-3 rationale explaining why string-literal preservation is load-bearing."
- 2026-05-25T05:50:23Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5d428ab` - "test(orchestrator): external-config audit invariants AC1-3 + AC4-5 findings (#11837) (#11946)

* test(orchestrator): external-config audit invariants AC1-3 + AC4-5 documented findings (#11837)

Sub 5 of Epic #11831 — external-user configurability audit (forks, npx neo-app workspaces, cloud tenants). V-B-A grep across ai/daemons/ confirms the substrate is already clean for AC1-3; this PR ships invariant tests that lock the clean state with regression-detection so future-agent changes can't silently re-introduce Neo-team-specific assumptions.

New spec file Orchestrator.externalConfig.spec.mjs (3/3 pass) covering:

**AC1 — No Neo-team identity literals (@neo-opus-4-7 / @neo-gpt / @neo-gemini-3-1-pro)** across the ai/daemons/ subtree (includes the orchestrator-daemon entrypoint at ai/daemons/orchestrator/daemon.mjs post-#11847 thematic split). Daemon code must resolve identities via the ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs registry, never via hardcoded literals.

**AC2 — No hardcoded "Project 12" / "v13" / "release:v*" literals** in daemon code. These don't survive Neo-release-cycle transitions or external deployments.

**AC3 — No operator absolute paths** (/Users/Shared/github/neomjs/neo or /Users/tobiasuhlig). All paths must resolve relative to checkout root via existing Neo path helpers.

The walker recurses ai/daemons/ skipping node_modules, dist, .git, .neo-ai-data; stripCommentsAndStrings inlines so JSDoc/comment-prose mentions of the patterns don't false-positive. String literals are preserved (exactly the surface AC1-3 specify scanning).

**AC4 — Deployment-profile honoring** verified at audit-time: all Orchestrator enable-flag getters route through resolveDeploymentEnabled(key) which respects AiConfig.orchestrator.deploymentMode (Orchestrator.mjs:347-351). localOnly substrate populated symmetrically per daemon.mjs:229-261. Not turned into invariant test (the existing #11941 + #11940 PRs introduce additional helper functions like resolveCloudOnlyEnabled for the cloud-default-on lane class; a meta-invariant would need to know that both helpers are acceptable — out of scope for AC4 narrow framing).

**AC5 — `npx neo-app` workspace safety** requires spawning a fresh workspace and exercising daemon boot under missing-Neo-substrate conditions — too heavy for unit-test scope. Deferred to follow-up (will file ticket if not already tracked).

Test-spec-only PR. 3/3 PASS at branch head; ai/daemons substrate unchanged. Close-target as `Refs #11837` (not Resolves) until AC4/AC5 follow-up coverage lands.

* docs(test): correct stripCommentsAndStrings JSDoc to match string-preserving behavior (#11837)

Per @neo-gpt #11946 cycle-1 truth-in-code RA: the helper JSDoc claimed it strips string literals, but the implementation deliberately preserves them because AC1-3 specifically target string-literal occurrences (e.g., '@neo-opus-4-7' as a literal is a violation, but a comment mentioning the same handle is not). Updated JSDoc to match implementation + added the AC1-3 rationale explaining why string-literal preservation is load-bearing.

- 2026-05-26T07:22:07Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by #12012
- 2026-05-26T07:40:05Z @tobiu referenced in commit `c36ff74` - "feat(orchestrator): add workspace-safety integration test (#11948) (#12011)

Sub-5 AC5 of #11837 — runtime workspace-safety probe PR #11946 deferred as too
heavy for unit-test scope. Adds two child-process spawn probes under
test/playwright/integration/ai/daemons/:

- AC3 cloud-mode boot: verifies the orchestrator daemon reaches the
  '[Orchestrator] Started.' log line in isolated NEO_AI_ORCHESTRATOR_DIR /
  NEO_AI_DB_PATH / NEO_HEARTBEAT_ALIVE_PATH paths under
  NEO_AI_DEPLOYMENT_MODE=cloud, with no UnhandledPromiseRejection / uncaught
  / init-failure surfaces.
- AC4 swarm-heartbeat resolver degrade-with-log: verifies the
  '[resolveSwarmHeartbeatTargets] selfIdentity is null — disabled' log line
  fires within a 30s pulse window in local mode with empty NEO_AGENT_IDENTITY
  + targetSource='self', proving the daemon boot path actually calls the
  resolver and survives the empty-substrate degrade gracefully.

Both probes spawn with cwd=workspaceDir to isolate cwd-relative side effects
(chroma data dir, child task PID files) from a concurrently-running operator
daemon. The sqlite DB is pre-initialized with the subset of
ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs::initSchema() the orchestrator's first open
requires, mimicking the post-MC-first-boot substrate shape an external user
would have.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
